text: Figure 13.4 Properties of the adjacency matrix. a) Example graph. b) Position $(m, n)$ of the adjacency matrix A contains the number of walks of length one from node $n$. c) Position $(m, n)$ of the squared adjacency matrix $\mathbf{A}^{2}$ contains the number of walks of length two from node $m$ to node $n$. d) One hot vector representing node six, which was highlighted in panel (a). e) When we pre-multiply this vector by $\mathbf{A}$, the result contains the number of walks of length two from node six to each node;
